course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
objective: Identify the regulatory framework for RCFEs.
question_text: The body of California regulations most directly governing RCFE operations is generally found in:
option_b: Federal tax code
rationale: RCFEs are licensed under California community care licensing regulations (Title 22). Confirm exact citations with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
objective: Clarify the renewal-level purpose of the course.
question_text: This RCFE continuing-education refresher is intended for administrators who are:
option_b: Renewing/maintaining their administrator certificate through continuing education
rationale: The course is a renewal-level CE refresher, not initial certification.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: An RCFE administrator's policies and procedures should be:
option_b: Written, current, accessible to staff, and consistent with applicable regulations
rationale: Current, accessible, regulation-aligned policies support compliant operations.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: RCFE administrators are responsible for ensuring the facility operates in compliance with applicable licensing regulations.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: The administrator is accountable for regulatory compliance.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: When a regulation and a facility's internal preference conflict, the administrator should:
option_b: Follow the applicable regulation and update internal practice
rationale: Applicable regulations take precedence over internal preferences.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: A licensing analyst arrives for an inspection. The administrator should:
option_b: Cooperate, provide access to required records, and respond professionally
rationale: Administrators cooperate with authorized licensing inspections and provide required access.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
objective: Explain the purpose of procedural documentation.
question_text: Procedural standards for documentation in an RCFE primarily exist to:
option_b: Demonstrate compliance, support resident safety, and provide an audit-ready record
rationale: Documentation standards support compliance, safety, and audit readiness.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: Scenario: An administrator discovers a facility practice that does not meet a current regulatory standard. The best response is to:
option_a: Continue the practice to avoid disruption
option_b: Correct the practice, update policy, document the corrective action, and train staff
rationale: Proactive correction, documentation, and staff training reflect sound compliance management.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: Posting of the facility license and required notices is generally:
option_b: Required and should be visible/accessible as specified by regulation
rationale: Required postings must be displayed per regulation. Confirm specifics with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
objective: Explain the value of regulatory currency.
question_text: Staying current with regulatory updates is important for an administrator because:
option_b: Requirements can change, and compliance must reflect current rules
option_c: It is only the staff's job
option_d: Updates do not affect the facility
rationale: Regulations evolve; administrators must keep current to remain compliant.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: Which are administrator responsibilities under licensing standards? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Ensuring staff meet training requirements
rationale: Records, staff training, and resident protection are responsibilities; falsification is prohibited.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: Which best describes 'procedural standards'?
option_b: Defined, consistent steps for carrying out required processes
rationale: Procedural standards define consistent steps for required processes.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: Maintaining required records for the retention period specified by regulation is part of regulatory compliance.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Record retention per regulation is a compliance requirement. Confirm retention periods with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
objective: Apply sound decision-making under regulatory uncertainty.
question_text: When the administrator is unsure how a specific regulation applies, the most defensible action is to:
option_b: Consult the regulation text and seek qualified guidance (e.g., licensing/legal) before acting
rationale: Consulting the source and qualified guidance supports compliant, defensible decisions.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-001 - Laws, Regulations, Policies, and Procedural Standards (4h)
question_text: The overall purpose of this laws-and-regulations refresher is to:
option_b: Reinforce current knowledge of the legal and procedural framework that governs RCFE operations
rationale: It reinforces current knowledge of the governing framework at a renewal level.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Person-centered dementia care is best described as care that:
option_a: Treats all residents with dementia the same
option_b: Focuses on the individual's history, preferences, abilities, and dignity
option_c: Prioritizes facility routines over the person
rationale: Person-centered dementia care individualizes support around the person's identity, preferences, and abilities.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
objective: Describe the nature of Alzheimer's disease.
question_text: Alzheimer's disease is best characterized as:
option_b: A progressive neurodegenerative condition affecting memory and cognition
rationale: Alzheimer's is a progressive neurodegenerative disease; it is not normal aging. Confirm clinical phrasing with SME.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Behavioral expressions in dementia (such as agitation) are best understood as:
option_b: Often a form of communication of an unmet need or discomfort
rationale: Behaviors often communicate unmet needs; identifying triggers is key to person-centered responses.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Validating a resident's feelings and using a calm approach can help reduce dementia-related distress.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Validation and calm communication are core person-centered techniques.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Which communication approach is most appropriate for a resident with moderate dementia?
option_b: Short, simple sentences, one step at a time, with patience
rationale: Simple, stepwise, patient communication supports comprehension and reduces distress.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: A meaningful activity program for residents with dementia should be:
option_b: Tailored to individual abilities, interests, and life history
rationale: Individualized, meaningful activity supports engagement and well-being.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Scenario: A resident with dementia becomes upset during the evening and repeatedly asks to 'go home.' A person-centered response is to:
option_a: Tell them repeatedly they live here now
option_b: Respond calmly, acknowledge the feeling, offer reassurance and redirection, and look for unmet needs (hunger, fatigue, pain)
option_c: Leave the resident alone until they stop
rationale: Acknowledge feelings, reassure, redirect, and assess for unmet needs (including possible 'sundowning' patterns).

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Which approaches support person-centered dementia care? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Maintaining familiar routines
rationale: Life story, routine, and family involvement support person-centered care; restraints for convenience do not.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Staff training on dementia in an RCFE should emphasize:
option_b: Understanding the disease, communication skills, and behavioral support strategies
rationale: Dementia training emphasizes understanding, communication, and behavioral support.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Which environmental feature can support residents with dementia?
option_b: Calm, predictable spaces with helpful cues and reduced overstimulation
rationale: Calm, cue-rich, low-overstimulation environments support orientation and comfort.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Preserving dignity for a resident with dementia during personal care includes:
option_b: Explaining steps simply, ensuring privacy, and offering choices when possible
option_d: Discussing the resident in front of others
rationale: Simple explanation, privacy, and choice preserve dignity.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: Family members can be valuable partners in understanding a resident's preferences and history.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Families provide insight into the resident's identity and preferences.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: A non-pharmacological first response to mild dementia-related agitation is generally to:
option_b: Identify and address triggers, reassure, and redirect
rationale: Non-pharmacological, trigger-focused responses are generally first-line; medication decisions are clinical. Confirm clinical guidance with SME.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
objective: Explain the value of caregiver consistency.
question_text: Why is consistency of caregivers helpful for residents with dementia?
option_b: Familiar caregivers build trust and reduce confusion and anxiety
rationale: Consistent, familiar caregivers support trust and reduce distress.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-002 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Person-Centered Care Foundations (4h)
question_text: The overall aim of person-centered dementia foundations is to:
option_a: Standardize all residents into one routine
option_b: Support each resident's dignity, abilities, and quality of life
rationale: The aim is dignity, ability support, and quality of life for each individual.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: A primary safety concern for residents with dementia who wander is:
option_b: Elopement (leaving the facility unsafely) and related injury risk
rationale: Wandering raises elopement and injury risk, requiring environmental and care planning.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: A dementia-supportive physical environment generally includes:
option_b: Secure but non-institutional design, clear cues, good lighting, and reduced hazards
rationale: Safe, cue-rich, well-lit, hazard-reduced environments support residents with dementia.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Documentation related to a resident with dementia should:
option_b: Objectively record behaviors, interventions tried, and outcomes to inform care planning
option_d: Be skipped if the resident cannot read it
rationale: Objective documentation of behaviors and interventions supports individualized care and accountability.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Restraints (physical or chemical) should not be used for staff convenience or discipline in dementia care.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Restraints for convenience/discipline are prohibited; least-restrictive, person-centered approaches are required. Confirm regulatory specifics with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Fall risk is often elevated in residents with dementia because of:
option_b: Impaired judgment, gait/perception changes, and possible medication effects
rationale: Cognitive, perceptual, gait, and medication factors raise fall risk, requiring prevention measures.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: An effective approach to reduce dementia-related agitation in the environment is to:
option_a: Increase noise and activity at all times
option_b: Reduce overstimulation, maintain routine, and provide calm spaces
rationale: Calm, predictable, low-stimulation environments reduce agitation.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Scenario: A resident with dementia is found near an exit door appearing intent on leaving. The most appropriate response is to:
option_a: Physically block and scold the resident
option_b: Calmly approach, reassure, redirect to a safe activity, and document/report to update the safety plan
option_c: Allow them to leave the building
rationale: Calm redirection plus documentation and safety-plan updates address elopement risk without improper restraint.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Which measures help keep residents with dementia safe? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Hazard reduction and good lighting
rationale: Safety planning, hazard reduction, and fall prevention help; punitive seclusion is prohibited.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
objective: Explain the purpose of safety features.
question_text: Secured perimeters or alarmed exits in a dementia setting are intended to:
option_b: Support resident safety while maintaining dignity and least-restrictive practices
rationale: Safety features support protection while preserving dignity; they do not replace supervision. Confirm regulatory specifics with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
objective: Link behavior change to possible medical causes.
question_text: When a dementia-related behavior changes suddenly, staff should consider:
option_b: A possible underlying medical cause (pain, infection, medication) and report for assessment
rationale: Sudden behavior change may signal a medical issue and should be reported for assessment.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Care plans for residents with dementia should be:
option_b: Individualized and updated as needs and behaviors change
rationale: Care plans should be individualized and updated to remain relevant.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Clear documentation of dementia-related incidents supports both resident safety and regulatory accountability.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Incident documentation supports safety and accountability.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: Adequate lighting and contrast in dementia environments help to:
option_b: Reduce confusion, misperception, and fall risk
rationale: Good lighting/contrast reduce misperception and falls.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: A least-restrictive approach to dementia safety means:
option_a: Using the most restrictive option first
option_b: Choosing the option that protects safety while preserving the most freedom and dignity
rationale: Least-restrictive means balancing safety with freedom and dignity.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-003 - Alzheimer's/Dementia: Safety, Environment, Documentation (4h)
question_text: The overall focus of this course (dementia safety, environment, and documentation) is to:
option_b: Strengthen safe environments, risk management, and accurate documentation for residents with dementia
rationale: The focus is safe environments, risk management, and documentation.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Residents in an RCFE have the right to:
option_a: Be treated with dignity, respect, and freedom from abuse
option_b: Be excluded from decisions about their care
option_c: Have personal mail opened by staff routinely
rationale: Resident rights include dignity, respect, and freedom from abuse and mistreatment.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
objective: Describe the role of resident councils.
question_text: A resident council primarily exists to:
option_b: Give residents a voice in facility matters affecting them
rationale: Resident councils provide a collective voice for residents.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Preserving resident dignity during personal care includes:
option_b: Discussing the resident loudly in common areas
rationale: Privacy and respect preserve dignity.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Residents generally have the right to participate in decisions about their own care and daily life.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Self-determination and participation are core resident rights.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Which is an example of protecting a resident's right to privacy?
option_a: Sharing care details with unauthorized visitors
option_b: Knocking before entering and keeping records confidential
rationale: Privacy includes confidentiality and respectful entry practices.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Abuse prevention in an RCFE is supported primarily by:
option_b: Staff screening, training, supervision, and a clear reporting culture
rationale: Screening, training, supervision, and reporting culture prevent abuse.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Scenario: A resident reports that a staff member spoke to them harshly and ignored their requests. The administrator should:
option_b: Take the complaint seriously, investigate per policy, protect the resident, and document/report as required
option_c: Tell the resident to stop complaining
rationale: Complaints must be taken seriously, investigated, and addressed with resident protection and documentation. Confirm reporting obligations with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Which are recognized resident rights? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Right to voice grievances without retaliation
option_c: Right to be restrained for convenience
option_d: Right to participate in care decisions
rationale: Dignity, grievance rights, and participation are rights; restraint for convenience is not.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Retaliation against a resident for filing a grievance is:
option_b: Prohibited; residents may voice concerns without fear of retaliation
rationale: Retaliation for grievances is prohibited.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Cultural competency in resident rights means:
option_b: Respecting diverse backgrounds, identities, and preferences in care
rationale: Cultural competency respects diversity, including identity and preferences.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: A resident's personal funds or property, when managed by the facility, must be:
option_b: Handled with accountability, accurate records, and protection from misuse
rationale: Resident funds/property require accountability and protection from exploitation. Confirm specific safeguards with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Residents generally have the right to receive visitors and communicate privately, subject to reasonable safety policies.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Visitation and private communication are rights, balanced with reasonable safety. Confirm current policy with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: The role of the Long-Term Care Ombudsman generally includes:
option_b: Advocating for residents and helping resolve concerns/complaints
rationale: The Ombudsman advocates for residents and assists with complaint resolution. Confirm role specifics with SME.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: Protecting dignity during group activities includes:
option_b: Offering choices and respecting a resident's decision to participate or not
rationale: Choice and respect for participation decisions preserve dignity.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-004 - Resident Rights, Dignity, Councils, Abuse Prevention (2h)
question_text: The overall purpose of this course is to:
option_b: Reinforce resident rights, dignity, council participation, and abuse prevention
rationale: The purpose is reinforcing rights, dignity, councils, and abuse prevention.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: In an RCFE, 'assistance with self-administration' of medication generally means staff may:
option_b: Help a resident take their own medication (e.g., reminders, opening containers) within regulatory limits
rationale: RCFE staff assist with self-administration within regulatory limits; they do not prescribe or make clinical decisions. Confirm exact scope with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: Centrally stored medications in an RCFE should be:
option_b: Stored securely, properly labeled, and accounted for per regulation
rationale: Secure storage, proper labeling, and accountability are required. Confirm specifics with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
objective: Explain the purpose of medication records.
question_text: Accurate medication records (such as a medication administration/assistance record) are important because they:
option_b: Document what was given/assisted, support safety, and provide an audit trail
rationale: Accurate records support safety and provide an audit trail.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
objective: Apply scope-of-practice limits to medication oversight.
question_text: An RCFE administrator's oversight of medication processes does not authorize unlicensed staff to perform tasks reserved for licensed professionals.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Oversight does not expand scope; licensed tasks remain with licensed professionals. Confirm scope with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: If a medication error or discrepancy is discovered, the appropriate response is to:
option_b: Follow facility policy: ensure resident safety, notify the appropriate licensed/health professional, and document/report
rationale: Errors require safety-first response, notification, and documentation per policy.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: Proper labeling of a resident's medication generally includes:
option_b: Resident name, medication, dose/directions, and other required pharmacy label elements
option_c: Only the color of the pill
rationale: Proper labels include resident, medication, and directions per pharmacy/regulatory standards.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
objective: Respond to changes in self-administration ability.
question_text: Scenario: A resident who self-administers medications appears increasingly confused and is making errors with their pills. The administrator should:
option_b: Report the change to the resident's physician/appropriate professional, review the needs/services plan, and adjust support within regulation
rationale: A change in ability triggers reassessment, professional notification, and plan adjustment within regulatory limits.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: Which support safe medication processes in an RCFE? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Accurate records
option_c: Allowing unlicensed staff to make dosing decisions
option_d: Staff training appropriate to their role
rationale: Secure storage, accurate records, and role-appropriate training are correct; unlicensed dosing decisions are not.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: Psychotropic medications in older adults require particular attention because they:
option_b: Can carry significant risks and should be used appropriately with monitoring and oversight
option_c: Should be used for staff convenience
rationale: Psychotropics carry risks and require appropriate use, monitoring, and oversight; not for convenience. Confirm clinical/regulatory guidance with SME.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: Disposal of medications should follow:
option_b: Facility policy and applicable regulations for safe, documented disposal
rationale: Disposal follows policy and regulation; sharing medications between residents is prohibited.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: Medications prescribed for one resident must never be given to another resident.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Medications are resident-specific and must not be shared.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
question_text: Staff who assist with medications should be trained to:
option_b: Follow proper procedures, recognize and report concerns, and stay within their role
rationale: Training emphasizes correct procedures, reporting, and staying within role.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
objective: Apply the right to refuse medication.
question_text: A resident's right to refuse medication generally means staff should:
option_b: Respect the refusal, document it, and notify the appropriate professional
rationale: Refusals are respected, documented, and reported for follow-up. Confirm process with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
objective: Explain the value of pharmacy/prescriber coordination.
question_text: Coordination with the pharmacy and prescribing professional helps ensure:
option_b: Correct, current medication information and safe processes
rationale: Coordination supports accurate, current information and safety.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-005 - Medication Management in RCFE Operations (4h)
objective: Clarify the scope of the medication course.
question_text: This course addresses medication management at the level of:
option_a: Certifying nurses to administer IV medications
option_b: Administrator oversight and safe RCFE medication processes (not medication-administration certification)
rationale: The course covers oversight and safe processes, not administration certification or prescribing.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
objective: Explain the purpose of pre-admission appraisal.
question_text: A pre-admission appraisal of a prospective resident is used to:
option_b: Determine whether the facility can meet the person's needs before admission
rationale: The appraisal determines whether the facility can meet the prospective resident's needs.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
objective: Describe needs and services plan requirements.
question_text: A needs and services plan should:
option_b: Be individualized, based on assessment, and updated as needs change
rationale: Needs and services plans are individualized and updated with reappraisal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: Reappraisal of a resident is generally triggered by:
option_b: A significant change in the resident's condition or needs (and per required intervals)
rationale: Reappraisal occurs with significant changes and at required intervals. Confirm intervals with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: A facility should not admit or retain a resident whose needs it cannot safely meet.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Admission/retention must align with the facility's ability to meet needs safely. Confirm specific retention criteria with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: Documentation supporting admission generally includes:
option_b: A physician's report/health assessment and required admission documents
rationale: Admission requires a health assessment/physician report and required documents. Confirm specifics with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: When a resident's needs increase beyond the facility's capacity, the administrator should:
option_b: Reassess, attempt appropriate accommodations within regulation, and plan a safe transition if needs cannot be met
rationale: Reassessment and, if needed, a safe, lawful transition protect the resident. Confirm process with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: Scenario: A resident's mobility declines and they now need more assistance than the current plan reflects. The administrator should:
option_b: Initiate reappraisal, update the needs and services plan, involve the resident/family and appropriate professionals, and document
rationale: A change in needs prompts reappraisal, plan update, involvement of the resident/family, and documentation.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: Which belong in a needs and services plan process? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Resident/family involvement
rationale: Assessment, involvement, and updates are required; ignoring preferences is not.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
objective: Explain the value of resident involvement.
question_text: Involving the resident (and family/representative as appropriate) in care planning supports:
option_b: Person-centered, individualized, and defensible care decisions
rationale: Involvement supports person-centered, individualized, defensible planning.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: Retention decisions should be based primarily on:
option_a: The resident's ability to pay only
option_b: Whether the facility can safely and lawfully meet the resident's assessed needs
option_d: How long the resident has stayed
rationale: Retention hinges on the facility's ability to safely and lawfully meet needs.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: The needs and services plan should be accessible to staff who provide the resident's care.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Care staff need access to the plan to deliver appropriate care.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
objective: Explain the purpose of a complete admission process.
question_text: A complete admission process helps the facility:
option_b: Confirm it can meet needs, set expectations, and establish a documented baseline
rationale: A complete admission process confirms fit, sets expectations, and documents a baseline.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: If required admission documentation is incomplete, the administrator should:
option_b: Obtain the required documentation to complete the record per regulation
rationale: Missing documentation must be obtained to complete the compliant record; never falsified.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: Reappraisal documentation should capture:
option_b: The changed needs, updated services, and who was involved
rationale: Reappraisal records should capture changes, updated services, and involvement.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-006 - Admission, Retention, Reappraisal, Needs and Services Plans (3h)
question_text: The overall purpose of this course is to:
option_b: Strengthen admission, retention, reappraisal, and needs-and-services planning practices
rationale: The purpose is sound admission, retention, reappraisal, and planning practices.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: An RCFE emergency/disaster plan should:
option_a: Exist only on paper and never be practiced
option_b: Be written, current, communicated to staff, and practiced through drills
rationale: Effective emergency plans are current, communicated, and practiced via drills.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Emergency drills in an RCFE are important because they:
option_b: Help staff and residents respond effectively and reveal gaps to fix
rationale: Drills build readiness and reveal gaps to correct.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Maintaining clear, unobstructed exits and pathways primarily supports:
option_b: Safe evacuation and fall prevention
rationale: Clear exits/pathways support safe evacuation and reduce falls.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Staff should know their roles and responsibilities during different types of emergencies.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Defined roles support a coordinated emergency response.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Emergency supplies (water, food, first aid, etc.) should be:
option_b: Maintained adequately and checked/replenished as needed
option_c: Stored where no one can reach them
rationale: Adequate, maintained emergency supplies support continuity during emergencies.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
objective: Plan for residents needing evacuation assistance.
question_text: When considering residents with mobility or cognitive impairments in an evacuation, the plan should:
option_b: Account for individual needs and assistance requirements
rationale: Plans must account for residents needing assistance during evacuation.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Scenario: A power outage occurs during extreme heat. The administrator should:
option_b: Activate the emergency plan, ensure resident safety and temperature/medical needs, communicate, and document actions
option_d: Ignore residents needing cooling or refrigerated medications
rationale: Activate the plan, protect residents (heat/medication needs), communicate, and document.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Which are part of physical-environment safety in an RCFE? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Clear, unobstructed exits
option_d: Safe water temperature and hazard control
rationale: Working alarms, clear exits, and hazard control are correct; blocked exits are a hazard.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Documentation of emergency drills should include:
option_b: Date, type of drill, participants, and any issues/corrective actions
rationale: Drill records should capture date, type, participation, and corrective actions.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Routine inspection/maintenance of safety equipment helps to:
option_b: Ensure equipment works when needed and reduce hazards
rationale: Maintenance ensures safety equipment functions and reduces hazards.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Knowing how and when to shut off utilities (e.g., gas, water) can be part of emergency preparedness.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Utility shutoff knowledge supports certain emergency responses.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: After an emergency, the administrator should:
option_b: Review the response, document outcomes, and update the plan to address gaps
rationale: Post-event review and plan updates strengthen future readiness.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: Communication during an emergency should include:
option_b: A plan for notifying staff, residents, families, and authorities as appropriate
rationale: A communication plan covers staff, residents, families, and authorities.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
objective: Explain the purpose of environmental safety.
question_text: A safe physical environment supports residents primarily by:
option_b: Reducing the risk of injury, supporting mobility, and enabling effective emergency response
rationale: A safe environment reduces injury risk and supports emergency response.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-007 - Emergency Procedures and Physical Environment Controls (2h)
question_text: The overall purpose of this course is to:
option_b: Strengthen emergency preparedness and physical-environment safety/controls in the RCFE
rationale: The purpose is emergency preparedness and environmental safety.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
objective: Define the purpose of staff supervision.
question_text: Effective staff supervision in an RCFE primarily aims to:
option_b: Ensure staff perform competently, safely, and consistently with policy and regulation
rationale: Supervision ensures competent, safe, policy-aligned performance.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: Staff training records should be:
option_b: Maintained, accurate, and demonstrate required training was completed
rationale: Accurate training records demonstrate compliance with training requirements.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: Administrator accountability means the administrator is responsible for:
option_b: Overall facility operations, compliance, staff oversight, and resident well-being
rationale: The administrator is accountable for operations, compliance, oversight, and resident well-being.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: New staff should receive orientation and required training before or promptly upon assuming their duties, per regulation.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Orientation/training requirements protect residents; confirm exact timelines with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
objective: Explain the value of continuing training.
question_text: Ongoing (continuing) staff training is important because it:
option_b: Maintains competency and keeps staff current with practices and regulations
rationale: Continuing training maintains competency and currency.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: When a staff member performs a task incorrectly, an accountable supervisory response is to:
option_b: Provide corrective feedback/retraining, document it, and monitor improvement
rationale: Corrective feedback, retraining, documentation, and monitoring are constructive and defensible.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: Scenario: A licensing review finds gaps in staff training documentation. The administrator should:
option_b: Identify the gaps, complete/verify required training, accurately document it, and implement a tracking system to prevent recurrence
rationale: Honest correction, completion of training, accurate documentation, and a tracking system are correct; falsification is prohibited.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: Which are administrator responsibilities for staff management? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Maintaining accurate personnel/training records
option_c: Allowing untrained staff to perform tasks beyond their preparation
rationale: Training, records, and supervision are responsibilities; allowing unprepared staff to perform tasks is not.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
objective: Explain the importance of adequate staffing.
question_text: Adequate staffing levels are important because they:
option_b: Support resident safety, needs, and quality of care
rationale: Adequate staffing supports safety and quality of care.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
objective: Explain the purpose of staff screening.
question_text: Background screening/clearance requirements for staff exist to:
option_b: Protect residents from individuals who may pose a risk
rationale: Screening helps protect residents; confirm specific clearance requirements with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: An administrator can delegate tasks but remains accountable for the facility's compliance and operations.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Delegation does not remove the administrator's overall accountability.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: Clear job descriptions and expectations help staff to:
option_b: Understand their roles, responsibilities, and limits
rationale: Clear roles support performance and accountability.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: A supportive supervision approach includes:
option_b: Clear expectations, training, feedback, and recognition
rationale: Effective supervision blends expectations, training, feedback, and recognition.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: Tracking training due dates and renewals helps the administrator to:
option_b: Stay compliant and ensure staff competencies are current
rationale: Tracking supports timely compliance and current competencies.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-008 - Staff Supervision, Training Records, Administrator Accountability (2h)
question_text: The overall purpose of this course is to:
option_b: Strengthen staff supervision, training-record management, and administrator accountability
rationale: The purpose is supervision, training records, and accountability.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
objective: Explain the purpose of business records.
question_text: Accurate business records in an RCFE support:
option_b: Compliance, financial accountability, and audit readiness
rationale: Accurate records support compliance, accountability, and audit readiness.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Records that must be retained should be kept:
option_b: For the period and in the manner required by applicable regulation
rationale: Retention follows regulatory requirements; confirm retention periods with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: 'Claim-safe' communication in operations generally means:
option_b: Communicating accurately and avoiding misleading or unsupported claims
rationale: Claim-safe communication is accurate and avoids misleading or unsupported statements.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Marketing and admission materials should accurately represent the services the facility actually provides.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Accurate representation avoids misleading prospective residents.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Resident financial records handled by the facility must be:
option_b: Kept accurate, separate as required, and protected from misuse
rationale: Resident funds require accuracy, required separation, and protection. Confirm specifics with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Confidentiality of resident and business information requires that information is:
option_b: Disclosed only to authorized parties and protected from improper access
rationale: Confidential information is protected and shared only with authorized parties.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Scenario: A prospective resident's family asks whether the facility provides a level of skilled care it does not actually offer. The administrator should:
option_a: Imply the facility offers it to secure the admission
option_b: Accurately describe the services the facility provides and not make unsupported claims
option_d: Promise to add the service without basis
rationale: Accurate, claim-safe communication prevents misrepresentation and protects residents.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Which support sound RCFE business operations? (Select all that apply.)
option_b: Proper retention of required records
rationale: Accurate records, retention, and confidentiality are sound; misleading claims are not.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: When required records are requested during an inspection, the administrator should:
option_b: Provide the required records in an organized, accurate, and timely manner
rationale: Required records should be provided accurately and timely; falsification is prohibited.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
objective: Explain the role of admission agreements.
question_text: A clear, written admission agreement helps to:
option_b: Set transparent expectations about services, fees, and terms
rationale: A clear admission agreement sets transparent expectations.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Backups and protection of important records help prevent loss of required documentation.
option_b: FALSE
rationale: Record protection/backups guard against loss of required documentation.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Fee changes or billing practices should be:
option_b: Communicated transparently and consistent with the agreement and regulation
rationale: Fees should be transparent and consistent with the agreement and regulation. Confirm specifics with SME/legal.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
objective: Explain the value of honest communication.
question_text: Honest, accurate communication with residents and families builds:
option_b: Trust and supports a defensible operation
rationale: Honest communication builds trust and supports defensibility.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: Organized recordkeeping systems help the administrator to:
option_b: Locate, maintain, and produce required records efficiently
rationale: Organized systems support efficient, compliant recordkeeping.

course_unit_module: RCFE-CETP-009 - Business Operations, Records, Claim-Safe Communications (2h)
question_text: The overall purpose of this course is to:
option_b: Strengthen business operations, recordkeeping, and claim-safe communications in the RCFE
rationale: The purpose is sound operations, recordkeeping, and claim-safe communication.
